page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96ls_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving top-tier status in Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), accelerates vector and matrix operations, which is reflected in strong prompt processing speeds during LLM inference benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with uncached read speeds reaching 257.01 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput is exceptionally high, placing in the top 10% of tested configurations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96ls_v6 offers high compute density and specialized instruction sets without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a highly efficient choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Recommended workloads include high-throughput web serving, software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores with a 4.2 GHz clock speed and 260 MB of L3 cache. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and contains no local storage or GPUs. It delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks, static web serving, and software compilation, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This profile makes the D96ls_v6 highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, CPU-bound tasks such as web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 is a compute-optimized instance with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v6 features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, optimized for high-compute and web serving workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and static web serving
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimized for external premium storage
Best suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based inference
